page: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 3 shared vCPUs mapped to AMD EPYC physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.3 GB of RAM per core, and a bundled 80 GB local SSD. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed profile. Single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% via stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores sit in the average tier. However, memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the PassMark memory score is poor, placing in the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth tests for both read and write operations consistently rank in the bottom 25%. This indicates that while individual CPU tasks execute efficiently, memory-intensive operations may experience latency.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio position this instance as a budget-friendly option for workloads with low-to-moderate resource demands. It is well-suited for lightweight applications such as basic text processing, single-threaded compression tasks, and small-scale LLM inference where prompt processing and text generation perform at average speeds. It is less optimal for memory-bound databases, software compilation, or high-throughput web serving due to the constrained memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the QEMU hypervisor, equipped with 3 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes 80 GB of local SSD storage but lacks dedicated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core CPU and general memory performance are weaker, with memory bandwidth and PassMark memory scores landing in the lower percentiles. This resource profile offers a cost-effective solution for lightweight, non-memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for basic text processing, single-threaded utility scripts, and small-scale language model inference, while being less optimal for high-throughput web serving or memory-heavy database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server with 3 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD storage. It offers strong single-core CPU performance for budget-friendly, lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server with 3 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD storage, optimized for budget workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-core virtual server featuring strong single-core performance and local SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: 3 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
4.0 GB system memory and 80 GB local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Constrained memory bandwidth and lower multi-core scaling
Suited for lightweight text processing and small-scale LLM inference
